danger_signals.visual_form: Bullet-shooting enemy spacecraft with distinct sprite designs
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion and destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Ground-based turret clusters forming boss arenas
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat defensive position; requires sustained attack within time limit

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy vessels with color-state indicators (blue → gold → pale yellow with red lines)
danger_signals.meaning: Heavily armored threat; damage state communicated through palette shifts

danger_signals.visual_form: Specific aircraft type distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Scout units; destroying them reduces game difficulty (ALC)
High defensive rating; standard weapons less effective

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen crowding with more simultaneous enemy sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Difficulty escalation triggered by player skill (ALC system)

safety_signals.meaning: Collectible benefit; grants weapon upgrades or specialty weapons

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue power-up boxes with numbered/color-coded visual variants
safety_signals.meaning: Weapon enhancement; increased firepower and bullet speed
Tactical options; grants shields, directional bullets, indestructible projectiles

safety_signals.visual_form: Point score accumulation reaching threshold; audio jingle triggers
safety_signals.meaning: Extra life gained; safety margin increased

safety_signals.visual_form: Player ship reappearing with temporary invulnerability period
safety_signals.meaning: Grace period after life loss; brief safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Screen load with HUD display and area number indication
safety_signals.meaning: Safe spawn point; new level checkpoint

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Space stations and planets defended; sole human presence on screen

faction_indicators.color_identity: Gold/yellow/red (mini-boss palette progression); varied enemy colorschemes
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fortress structures; organized armada formations; part-organic/part-mechanical aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types (aircraft, turrets, disks, reconnaissance planes); color shifts indicate damage state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortified space stations, planetary defense installations, armada concentrations

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player performing well (high skill, frequent shooting, power-up collection)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy density and sprite count on screen; more aggressive formations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy AI (ALC) escalates difficulty; tougher enemy types deploy more frequently

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player performing poorly (lives lost, new level started, reconnaissance planes destroyed)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Reduced enemy spawn rate; fewer simultaneous sprites on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy AI (ALC) decreases difficulty; less aggressive attack patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Capital ship color transitions: blue → gold → pale yellow with red lines
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visual health indicator; guides player perception of damage progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stationary turret cluster arrangement; time-limit counter appears in HUD
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Turrets maintain defensive positions; destruction bonus awarded for completion within time

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen transition to game over display; alternate audio cue (NES version with Select hold)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continue option available; player can restart from lost level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Area number display on screen; themed environment change (planets, space stations, outer space)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New enemy patterns; System defenses change per area

environmental_storytelling.example: 12 levels traverse various planets, space stations, and outer space—visual representation of player advancing deeper into System's territory
Stationary turret fortresses indicate System's entrenched defensive positions; required time-limited destruction suggests urgency and stakes
Meteors, reconnaissance planes, and turrets in early areas suggest outer defense layers; armored ships in later areas indicate core defenses
Mini-boss color transitions (blue → gold → red) visually communicate progression toward System core destruction without dialogue
Area number display and time limits on fortress objectives communicate mission structure and System's organized defense hierarchy

environmental_storytelling.technique: Power-up box frequency as difficulty pacing
environmental_storytelling.example: Blue descending boxes appear more frequently in balanced difficulty zones; scarcity signals high-danger areas

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive to input; fires main cannon continuously; can change specialty weapons; accumulates power-ups
Invincibility period active; all power-ups dropped; temporary evasion advantage
Fires projectiles at player; moves in formations; requires destruction
Destruction reduces ALC difficulty; acts as skill-check opponent
Bullets ricochet off hull; color shifts with damage accumulation; high resilience
Defend fixed positions; fire in patterns; destruction required within time limit for bonus; indicates System's tactical organization
Monitors player actions (attack frequency, power-up collection, boss completion time); escalates/reduces enemy threat dynamically

notes: Zanac's manifestation layer centers on adaptive difficulty communicated through enemy density and type distribution rather than explicit world-state UI. The System is visualized as an organized, escalating defensive hierarchy across 12 levels. Mini-boss palette transitions (blue→gold→red) are the primary visual health indicator—a design pattern that influenced later NES shooters. The game's world-state (player skill level) is communicated implicitly through enemy behavior changes driven by the ALC system, making player agency feel mechanically responsive. Regional differences (NES vs FDS) include mini-boss color persistence changes, indicating different visual communication strategies between versions. The ending sequence contains a hidden message referencing related Compile titles, suggesting a larger narrative universe not directly manifested in gameplay visuals.
